title: Factory Task Contract v2
description: Canonical, signed cross-factory task contract (RFC-0002). Superset of AIFactory's implementation_plan.json adding execution + tfactory profiles. PFactory produces it; AIFactory executes it (skip planning); TFactory tests from it.

contract_version.description: Contract schema version. '2' enables the execution/tfactory blocks.

correlation_key.description: RFC-0001 correlation key: the GitHub issue number as a string, or a synthetic <prefix>-<id>.

base_ref.description: RFC-0010 reconnaissance: the branch/tag the plan was built against, e.g. 'main'.

baseline_commit.description: RFC-0010 reconnaissance: the resolved commit SHA the RepoMap was built from. Lets AIFactory/TFactory anchor to the exact tree the human approved and detect drift.

approval.description: HMAC trust envelope (AIFactory trusted_plan). Required for the skip-planning fast-path.

signature.description: HMAC-SHA256 hex over the canonical contract + approval metadata.

change_mode.description: RFC-0010 reconnaissance verdict, distinct from workflow_type (which is the work-shape). greenfield => new code, no existing repo grounding. modify => edit existing code in place (the repo's language wins). migration => rewrite from source_language to target_language (the only case a language change is legitimate). Absent => greenfield (back-compat).

required_commands.description: Global verification command NAMES granted into the security allowlist (uv/pytest/ruff/mypy/...).

depends_on.description: Phase numbers, or planner slugs (e.g. 'phase-1-scaffold'), this phase depends on.

execution.description: HOW to execute — computed by PFactory, consumed by AIFactory. Absent => AIFactory uses its own defaults/planning.

model.description: Default model (full id or shorthand opus/sonnet/haiku).

phase_models.description: Per-phase model overrides keyed by spec|planning|coding|qa|qa_fixer.

parallel.description: Whether AIFactory may build this task's independent subtasks CONCURRENTLY. PFactory derives it from the plan's dependency shape: true when some phase holds more than one subtask at the same dependency level (a phase marked parallel_safe), false for a pure dependency chain. AIFactory honors it via task_metadata.parallel -> parallel_runner, which runs each concurrent subtask in its OWN child git worktree over a disjoint file set and merges sequentially. Absent => AIFactory's own default (serial).

workers.description: Maximum subtasks AIFactory may build concurrently per wave. PFactory sets it to the WIDEST phase's subtask count, capped at 4 -- a planner-side policy, not a contract invariant, so the schema deliberately imposes no maximum. Only consulted when parallel is true; workers <= 1 makes a phase ineligible for parallel execution. Absent => AIFactory's own default (3).

autonomy_tier.description: RFC-0011 label-driven difficulty tier (from factory:low|medium|hard). Drives model/planning/human-gate/verification/merge: low => cheap/local model (ollama->haiku), skip planning, no human, auto-merge when green; medium => sonnet, async human approval; hard => opus, PFactory planning, blocking human approval, highest verification (+equivalence lane for a rewrite). Precedence: highest wins; change_mode=migration forces hard. Absent => derived from complexity (back-compat).

skip_planning.description: When true AND signature+completeness verify, AIFactory goes straight to the wave executor.

budget_usd.description: OPTIONAL soft cost budget in USD for the task. OBSERVE-ONLY: when AIFactory's rolled-up spend exceeds it, the terminal completion event carries a usage.budget warning block (limit_usd/spent_usd/exceeded) and an OTel budget.exceeded metric fires. NEVER aborts, pauses, or kills the build. Absent => no budget tracking (back-compat).

routing.description: RFC-0014 cost-aware, capability-aware routing decision. Emitted by PFactory's cost router AFTER the RFC-0011 tier floor is set; records WHY the per-role models were chosen and the cost envelope they fit. Fully additive and OPTIONAL — absent => today's behaviour (contract_version stays "2"). Open object; consumers ignore unknown keys. The router never drops below the tier/class capability floor: cost is minimized only within that envelope.

class.description: Routing class from the task scorer (RFC-0014 §4). 'governed' (high risk / production) forces a frontier model for PLANNING even when coding/testing are cheap; it is never silently degraded.

cost_estimate_usd.description: Pre-execution rolled-up cost estimate in USD for the chosen per-role models. null when the selection is subscription- or local-priced (no $ estimate — costed as tokens/time, mirroring CFactory billing-mode display).

cost_ceiling_usd.description: The cost ceiling the router targeted. The chosen selection keeps cost_estimate_usd under this ceiling where a $ estimate exists; null => no ceiling applied.

policy.description: The operator policy that governed the selection, e.g. 'operator-default'.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of the per-role picks and any downgrades/holds (e.g. 'tier=medium floor=sonnet; planning kept at opus by policy; qa/test_gen downgraded to cheap within floor').

runtime.description: RFC-0014 execution runtime. Default 'claude' (today's path, unchanged). All non-'claude' runtimes are GATED OFF unless the operator enables them in config/env allowlist AND the contract opts in. 'claude-subagents' and 'dynamic-workflow' are the speed-up runtimes (parallel sub-agent fan-out / scripted multi-agent orchestration) and are manual-enable only because they multiply spend. Absent => 'claude'.

budget_mode.description: RFC-0014 budget enforcement mode. 'observe' (default) keeps budget_usd as a post-hoc warning that never changes the run. 'enforce' (operator-gated) lets the router refuse a selection whose estimate exceeds the ceiling — downgrading within the capability floor, or failing fast with a named reason if even the floor exceeds it. A 'governed' task is never silently degraded. Absent => 'observe'.

tfactory.description: VERIFY profile — computed by PFactory, carried by AIFactory, consumed by TFactory. Absent => TFactory infers.

frameworks.description: Lane -> framework, e.g. {unit: pytest, browser: playwright}.

docker_compose.description: Path to a compose file for integration lanes.

security_scope.description: Rule sets, e.g. owasp:* ; empty => out of scope.

equivalence.description: RFC-0010 behavioral-equivalence profile for change_mode=migration. The differential lane runs the SAME golden corpus against the legacy source impl (reference oracle) and the new target impl, asserting parity. Absent => no equivalence testing.

golden_corpus_ref.description: Path to the golden-corpus MANIFEST (input vectors declared by PFactory). Expected outputs are captured by TFactory by running the legacy source in its hermetic sandbox — NEVER by the planner. The captured corpus is content-hash addressed so the differential lane proves the same corpus ran against both impls.

parity_threshold.description: Minimum matching-vector ratio to pass (e.g. 1.0). Vectors flagged critical must all match regardless.

differential_lanes.description: Which lanes are run differentially (source-vs-target) rather than against the target alone.

module_map.description: source_module -> target_module mapping driving the rewrite and the per-module parity report.

epic_context.description: Sanitized enrichment carried alongside the plan for the downstream agents (constraints, knowledge links, and RFC-0012 house standards). Open object; consumers ignore unknown keys.

content_trust.description: Untrusted-content provenance marking for prompt-injection defense (docs/security/untrusted-content-threat-model.md, issue #273). PFactory sets it at intake; every downstream service carries it through unchanged and wraps untrusted text in the canonical delimiter before prompt injection. Fully additive and OPTIONAL - ABSENT => consumers MUST assume untrusted_user_content (fail-closed default); the block exists to positively assert trust, never to let absence imply it.

default.description: Trust class of the contract's free-text fields as a whole. 'untrusted_user_content' for anything derived from issue text, submitted specs, repo content, or MCP responses; 'trusted' ONLY for operator-authored input.

sources.description: Per-origin trust detail for mixed contracts.

constitution.description: RFC-0015 §3.1 constitution: the per-project, human-authored governing principles (repo .factory/constitution.md, mirrored to Backstage) every phase is checked against. Additive to RFC-0012 house_standards: PFactory injects it into planning/readiness, AIFactory coder/QA honour it, and the standards_conformance gate treats clauses tagged enforceable=true as HARD checks (closing spec-kit's soft-enforcement gap). Retrieval degrades, never fakes: available=false => the constitution was unavailable and the gate scores it not_applicable (never a false pass). Absent => today's behaviour.

source.description: Where the constitution came from, e.g. '.factory/constitution.md' or a Backstage entity ref.

principles.description: The governing principles, one entry each.

id.description: Stable principle id, e.g. 'P1', referenced by enforceable_ids and gate findings.

text.description: The principle statement (testing policy, architecture constraint, quality bar, security posture).

enforceable.description: true => the standards_conformance gate treats this clause as a HARD check; false => advisory, injected into prompts only.

enforceable_ids.description: The ids of the principles tagged enforceable=true — the subset the standards_conformance gate enforces as hard checks.

available.description: false when the constitution could not be retrieved (no .factory/constitution.md, Backstage unreachable). The gate then scores it not_applicable — never a false pass.

house_standards.description: RFC-0012 external-knowledge grounding: the team's house standards retrieved (best-effort) from RFC-0010 repo conventions and the live Backstage catalog/TechDocs/scaffolder templates, recorded so the fleet can FOLLOW them and the standards_conformance gate can prove they were applied. Retrieval degrades, never raises: available=false => retrieval was unavailable and the gate scores it not_applicable (never a false pass). Each source carries a content-hash so a standard cannot be silently swapped after approval, and waivers bind to that hash.

available.description: false when the standards could not be retrieved (Backstage unreachable, no matching entity). A baseline-only manifest may still be available=true.

error.description: Why retrieval was unavailable, when available=false.

sources.description: The retrieved standards, one entry per source.

source.description: 'baseline' = RFC-0010 repo conventions (always offline-available); 'backstage' = pulled from the live catalog.

content_hash.description: Stable digest (sha256:...) of the source-of-truth. The conformance gate and any deviation waiver bind to this hash.

techdocs_refs.description: TechDocs ref URIs the agents must consult (refs only, not inlined bodies).

tags.description: Catalog/template tags (used to match the golden-path stack).

deployment.description: RFC-0013 deployment-aware planning: what PFactory discovered about how this change ships (CI system, deploy mechanism, target environments), the risk/scan/system-gate policy that follows from it, best-effort DORA context from the deployment-metrics MCP, and the DRY-RUN deploy-verification policy. Fully additive and OPTIONAL — absent => the task has no deployment dimension (contract_version stays "2"). Open object; consumers ignore unknown keys. Discovery degrades, never fabricates: dora_context.available=false means metrics were unreachable, NOT that delivery is healthy. Production deploys are VAL-4 and NEVER autonomous.

ci_system.description: The CI provider detected in the target repo. 'none' => no CI configuration was found.

ci_exists.description: true when a CI pipeline definition already exists in the repo (so the change rides existing CI rather than needing one created).

ci_pipeline_paths.description: Paths to the discovered CI pipeline files, e.g. ['.github/workflows/deploy.yml'].

needs_pipeline.description: true when the task requires a CI/CD pipeline to be created or extended (no usable pipeline exists for this change).

deploy_system.description: The deploy mechanism detected. PaaS targets (gcp-cloud-run/azure-container-apps/aws-app-runner) may be resolved from plan text. 'unknown' => deployment indicators present but mechanism unresolved; 'none' => no deployment surface.

deploy_target_environments.description: Environments this change can reach, e.g. ['dev', 'staging', 'production'].

managed_services.description: Managed data services a PaaS deploy provisions (RFC-0013). Empty/absent => none detected.

production_classification.description: Highest-blast-radius environment class this change touches. 'production' forces the VAL-4 / never-autonomous deploy policy.

risk_class.description: Deployment risk tier derived from blast radius, production_classification, and destructive_iac. Drives required_scans and system_gates floors.

required_scans.description: Security/quality scans that MUST run before this change can deploy, e.g. ['sast', 'dependency-audit', 'iac-scan', 'secret-scan'].

system_gates.description: Named pre-deploy gates that MUST pass, e.g. ['ci-green', 'human-approval', 'change-window']. Production carries 'human-approval' (never autonomous).

dora_context.description: Best-effort DORA delivery context from the deployment-metrics MCP (deploy_history / dora_metrics tools). Degrades, never fabricates: available=false => metrics were unreachable and downstream policy treats delivery health as UNKNOWN, never healthy.

source.description: Where the metrics came from, e.g. 'stub', 'github-deployments', 'datadog'.

available.description: false when DORA metrics could not be retrieved. Numeric fields are then null.

deploy_success_rate.description: Recent deploy success ratio with its window, e.g. {"value": 0.94, "window_days": 30, "sample": 50}.

lead_time_p50_hours.description: Median lead time for change (commit to deploy) in hours. null when unavailable.

change_fail_rate.description: Change failure rate (0..1). null when unavailable.

last_deploy.description: The most recent deploy seen, e.g. {"env": "production", "status": "success", "at": "2026-06-18T10:00:00Z"}.

readiness.description: Deploy-readiness checklist. Every check that did not pass MUST carry a reason (honest-reporting, mirrors $defs.verification). A 'fail'/'not_run' without a reason is invalid.

reason.description: Why not passed — REQUIRED for fail/not_run.

action.description: Remediation the planner recommends to clear this check.

deploy_verification.description: The DRY-RUN deploy-verification policy for this change. Production deploys are VAL-4 and NEVER executed autonomously — verification stops at a dry-run/plan (VAL-2/VAL-3) and the real production apply requires the system_gates human-approval.

mode.description: How far deploy verification goes. 'dry-run'/'plan-only' for non-prod automation; 'ephemeral-apply' against a disposable target (VAL-3); 'production-apply' (VAL-4) is gated and never autonomous.

valLevel.description: Verification Assurance Level (RFC-0006). VAL-0 static · VAL-1 unit · VAL-2 ephemeral integration · VAL-3 sandbox target · VAL-4 production (never autonomous).

verification.description: RFC-0006 honest-reporting block. A status is reported ONLY at achieved_level; every un-run/failed level carries a reason. A producer that omits this block is treated as VAL-0 (never 'tested'). The ordinal never-overclaim rule (no level marked 'passed' above achieved_level) is enforced by the verification gate (scripts/verification_gate.py), not expressible in JSON Schema alone.

reason.description: Why not passed — REQUIRED for failed/not_run/skipped.

risk.description: Plain-language consequence of this gap for the user.

claim.description: Mandatory honest one-liner stating what was and was NOT verified.

traceability.description: RFC-0015 §4 D2 requirement->test->VAL matrix: every acceptance criterion mapped to the test(s) that cover it, the VAL level reached, and the verdict. Surfaces the AC->test mapping the fleet already computes internally; consumed by the CFactory matrix view (AC x test x VAL x verdict). Additive/optional — absent => no traceability surfaced (a producer omitting it is not penalized; it is still VAL-0 unless the levels block says otherwise).

ac_text.description: The acceptance-criterion text (optional, for the human-readable matrix).

tests.description: Test ids/paths covering this AC, e.g. 'tests/test_login.py::test_ok'. Empty => the AC has no covering test (a traceability gap).

status.description: Verdict of this AC's covering tests.

access.description: RFC-0007 access requirements discovered at planning time. Absent => the task needs no external/authenticated resource. The curation gate sets curated/human_approval; TFactory consumes credential_ref; the VAL gate (RFC-0006) treats an un-curated requirement as a VAL-3 not_run. Secrets are NEVER stored here — only broker refs.

requirements.description: One entry per external/authenticated resource the task's tests must reach.

auth_class.description: A: OIDC federation / service account / scoped token (no MFA in path). B: device-code / refresh token / TOTP-seed-as-secret / captured session (human clears MFA once). C: disposable target the pipeline owns (no prod cred). D: interactive MFA (push / hardware key / SMS) — refused or assisted, never faked.

credential_ref.description: Existing broker ref the test will use: env:NAME | vault:path#field | store:<id>. Never an inline secret.

env_required.description: Env var names the auth needs at runtime.

bootstrap.description: human => a one-time human-verified login/seed/approval is required before this is usable.

curated.description: Set true ONLY by the curation gate once the credential/target is provisioned and a liveness check passes. VAL-3 stays not_run until true.

human_approval.description: Audit trail of the human bootstrap (RFC-0001a chain).

scope.description: What was granted, e.g. 'sandbox account, read+deploy, auto-teardown'.

mvp_note.description: For auth_class D: the honest reason testing cannot pass this gate autonomously.

environment.description: RFC-0005 environment manifest — the single source of truth for the per-task toolchain, so the build env (AIFactory) and verify env (TFactory) cannot drift. The planner writes it; the provisioner materializes a throwaway sandbox from it (Tier A Nix preferred). Absent => the task runs in whatever the consumer's default runner provides.

language.description: Primary language under test, e.g. 'python', 'rust', 'go', 'typescript'. For change_mode=migration this is the TARGET language (kept populated for back-compat consumers that read only `language`).

source_language.description: RFC-0010 migration only: the language being ported FROM (the legacy reference impl), e.g. 'python'. Absent outside migration.

target_language.description: RFC-0010 migration only: the language being ported TO (the deliverable), e.g. 'rust'. Mirrors `language` for migration tasks.

toolchain.description: Pinned tool => version map, e.g. {"rust": "1.90", "cargo": "1.90"}. Versions feed the flake/devcontainer the provisioner builds.

system_packages.description: OS/Nix package names the tests need beyond the language toolchain, e.g. ['pkg-config', 'openssl-dev', 'chromium']. For a browser lane: the browser + its libs (e.g. via nixpkgs playwright-driver.browsers).

build_commands.description: Commands AIFactory runs to build the artifact in the materialized env.

verify_commands.description: Commands TFactory runs to verify in the SAME materialized env (no drift).

serve_command.description: How to start the app inside the materialized env for a lane that needs it running (browser/api against a live URL), e.g. 'python -m uvicorn app:app --port 8099'. Optional: when absent the consumer detects it from the checkout. Backgrounded by the runner, with a readiness wait, before the browser/api verify commands.

provisioning.description: How the consumer materializes this env. The provisioner resolves it tiered (A Nix, B image catalog, C on-demand build, D setup script).

method.description: nix => `nix develop -c` of `ref` (preferred, hermetic). devcontainer => devcontainer.json. image => a prebuilt registry image tag. setup_script => run `ref` inside the ephemeral container (last resort).

generated.description: true when the planner generated `ref` (and committed it as a deliverable) rather than the repo already carrying it.

network.description: Egress posture for the materialized sandbox. 'none' for hermetic build/unit; 'restricted' when the lane must reach a declared target (RFC-0007 access).

proof.description: Materialize-or-HALT evidence (RFC-0005 §3.4): commands that must succeed in the provisioned env before coding/verifying proceeds.

verify.description: Toolchain-presence checks, e.g. ['rustc --version', 'chromium --version']. A failure HALTs with a provisioning error, never a silent fallback.

routing.description: RFC-0014 v1 static per-stage tier routing. PFactory writes `requested` from the operator's versioned routing policy at plan-emit; each service fills `actual` at its own model seam for audit (CFactory shows requested-vs-actual). Fully additive and OPTIONAL — absent => no routing policy applied, today's behaviour (contract_version stays "2"). Open object; consumers ignore unknown keys. Precedence (highest first): pinned_model > overrides[stage] > RFC-0011 tier floor > policy default. A stage is never routed BELOW its RFC-0011 tier floor, and provider fallback stays within the same tier. Distinct from execution.routing (the v2 cost-router decision record): this block is the v1 policy request/audit carrier.

routing_policy_version.description: Version of the operator routing policy that produced `requested`, e.g. '2026-07-static-v1'. Lets a benchmark re-run pin exactly which policy it measured.

pinned_model.description: ESCAPE HATCH — wins over everything. When set, every stage uses exactly this model (full id or shorthand) and the routing policy is bypassed entirely. Preserves pre-RFC-0014 behaviour for pinned runs.

requested.description: Stage -> tier the policy requested, keyed by intake|planning|coding|qa_quick|qa_full|test_gen|triage (RFC-0014 §2b.1 defaults: intake/qa_quick=small, coding/test_gen=mid, planning/qa_full/triage=frontier). Written by PFactory.

overrides.description: Per-task stage -> tier overrides (same keys as `requested`). Beats the policy default but never the RFC-0011 tier floor or pinned_model.

actual.description: Stage -> concrete model id ACTUALLY used, filled by whichever service executed the stage (PFactory: intake/planning; AIFactory: coding/qa_quick/qa_full; TFactory: test_gen/triage). The audit trail for requested-vs-actual in CFactory.

baseline.description: RFC-0010 reconnaissance summary: what PFactory statically observed in the target repo and grounded the plan on. The planner reads code STATICALLY (AST/manifest/HCL parsing) and NEVER executes it. A stable digest of this block (commit + change_mode + resolved language + footprint) is folded into the approval content-hash, so the human approves a plan grounded in a known tree and approval invalidates if the repo drifts. Absent => the plan was not grounded in an existing repo (greenfield).

commit.description: Resolved commit SHA the RepoMap was built from.

available.description: false when the repo could not be read (private/unreachable/empty). Recon degrades to greenfield rather than failing the run.

error.description: Why recon was unavailable, when available=false.

versions.description: Pinned tool/runtime versions discovered from the repo, e.g. {"terraform": "1.7", "python": "3.11"}.

iac_resources.description: Static IaC resource inventory keyed by tool, e.g. {"terraform": {"resources": [{"type": "aws_eks_cluster", "name": "main", "file": "eks.tf"}], "modules": [...], "providers": [...]}}.

existing_test_command.description: Discovered test/check command, e.g. 'make test', 'uv run pytest'.

conventions.description: Linter/formatter/test-layout conventions to constrain generation (e.g. ruff, 4-space indent, tests/ layout).

dependency_graph.description: Best-effort intra-repo dependency edges (import graph for code; module source refs for IaC).

files.description: Files the plan will touch plus their reverse-dependents.

destructive_iac.description: IaC changes that force resource replacement/destruction (high-severity advisory).
